name: EA-MSFT Ontology (Microsoft Cloud Platform)
description: Microsoft Cloud platform ontology covering Azure resources and subscriptions, landing zones, Power Platform, Microsoft 365, Well-Architected Framework pillars and assessment criteria, and Azure AI service bindings. AI/ML entities extracted to EA-AI-ONT v1.0.0.

components.CloudPlatform: Cloud provider platform abstraction (Azure, future AWS/GCP)
components.AzureResource: Base class for Azure cloud resources
components.AzureSubscription: Azure billing and access control boundary
components.DesignPrinciple: WAF guiding principle for architecture decisions
components.AzureAIService: Azure AI service resource for AI workload deployment

crossOntology.name: hostedOnPlatform
crossOntology.purpose: EA-CORE -> EA-MSFT: Application hosting on cloud platform

crossOntology.name: assessedByPillar
crossOntology.purpose: EA-CORE -> EA-MSFT: Application WAF assessment coverage

crossOntology.name: deployedOnAzureAI
crossOntology.purpose: EA-AI -> EA-MSFT: AI workload deployment on Azure AI services

versionHistory.changes: Initial Microsoft Cloud platform ontology consolidated from V3 ea-azure, azure-waf, and azure-ai source material. 15 entities, 12 relationships, 8 business rules, 4 enumerations. OAA v6.1.0 compliant.

AI entity extraction to EA-AI-ONT. Removed 5 AI entities, 4 AI relationships, 2 AI enumerations, 3 AI rules. Added EA-AI import, AzureAIService entity, deployedOnAzureAI relationship, AzureAIServiceType enum. 11 entities, 9 relationships, 6 business rules, 3 enumerations.

notes: EA-MSFT provides Microsoft-specific cloud platform modelling extending EA-CORE and importing EA-AI for AI/ML capabilities. Covers Azure IaaS/PaaS resources, Power Platform, M365, Well-Architected Framework assessments, and Azure AI service bindings. AI entities are vendor-neutral in EA-AI-ONT; EA-MSFT binds them to Azure services via deployedOnAzureAI.
